RENAME_EXCL is atomic and no-clobber at the syscall level, closes the check-to-rename window, fails closed with EXDEV on a cross-device layout instead of degrading to copy+delete, and keeps one canonical promotion primitive in the file; `mv` can then be dropped from the required-command probe."},{"title":"Canonical-source shape validated only after the gigabyte-scale copy; space gate trusts the registered size","evidence":"scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:775-796 — the canonical Windows manifest is written and hash-verified fetched before the copy (:775-778), but `manifest_shape \"$win_manifest\"` is compared against `expected_shape` only after `scp -r` completes (:792-795); the free-space gate (:779-784) computes required_free from the registered `expected_shape` constant rather than from the already-fetched manifest.","predicted_failure":"A canonical Windows tree that no longer matches the registered shape — the neighboring class of the live-observed fixture defect this commit fixes (.review/findings/ldt-4.md records shape-identical-but-content-different; shape drift is the adjacent case) — is discovered only after copying up to the full fixture: the launch spends a 1 GiB-plus transfer before voiding, and in the oversize case scp can push q's free space below the 33 GB MIN_FREE_BYTES floor before failing, leaving an oversized partial tree retained in the session namespace. End state is still fail-closed, so impact is wasted transfer, host disk pressure, and operator cleanup burden in already-failing scenarios.","severity":"LOW","better_approach":"Validate the fetched canonical manifest before copying: compute `win_shape=$(manifest_shape \"$win_manifest\")` immediately after the fetch and session_void unless it equals `expected_shape \"$fixture\"`, then run the space gate (optionally sizing it from the observed manifest byte total, which now provably equals the registered constant) before invoking scp; keep the post-copy q-versus-Windows shape and byte-for-byte cmp checks unchanged."}]}

tool_input.description: List commits between base and head SHAs
Show diffstat between base and head

tool_input.command: git diff 4e0fdc307ba26e81f8532cd191089fa291c7f1aa ef48920720b02d09a490c1c07f6acd35651aba65 -- scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze.py scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze_test.py
tool_input.description: Show diff of the benchmark and analyzer scripts
Show HEAD SHA and the head blob hash of the harness script

findings.title: Fixture promotion uses mv -n instead of the harness's exclusive atomic rename primitive
findings.evidence: scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:800 promotes the validated incoming tree with `mv -n "$incoming" "$Q_STAGE_ROOT/fixtures/"`, while the same file already defines `rename_q_directory_exclusive` (scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:2062-2086, renameatx_np with RENAME_EXCL) and uses it at scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:2107 for the analogous no-clobber directory promotion in run retention. BSD mv falls back to copy-then-unlink on cross-device rename (EXDEV) instead of failing, and mv -n exits 0 when it skips an existing destination (covered here only by the pre/post existence checks at :798-803).
findings.predicted_failure: If the session root and staging root ever sit on different volumes (both are fixed constants under /Users/michael today, so this is latent), promotion silently becomes a non-atomic copy plus deletion of the validated incoming tree: an interruption mid-promotion leaves a partial tree at the stable fixture path — the exact poisoning the incoming-namespace design exists to prevent — which the next launch voids on manifest mismatch and which can only be cleared by a manual delete the harness itself refuses to perform; even the successful case unlinks the session-scoped source tree, contradicting the harness's documented no-deletion posture.
findings.better_approach: Promote with the existing primitive: `rename_q_directory_exclusive "$incoming" "$Q_STAGE_ROOT/fixtures/src_$fixture"`, keeping the surrounding existence checks. RENAME_EXCL is atomic and no-clobber at the syscall level, closes the check-to-rename window, fails closed with EXDEV on a cross-device layout instead of degrading to copy+delete, and keeps one canonical promotion primitive in the file; `mv` can then be dropped from the required-command probe.

findings.title: Canonical-source shape validated only after the gigabyte-scale copy; space gate trusts the registered size
findings.evidence: scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:775-796 — the canonical Windows manifest is written and hash-verified fetched before the copy (:775-778), but `manifest_shape "$win_manifest"` is compared against `expected_shape` only after `scp -r` completes (:792-795); the free-space gate (:779-784) computes required_free from the registered `expected_shape` constant rather than from the already-fetched manifest.
findings.predicted_failure: A canonical Windows tree that no longer matches the registered shape — the neighboring class of the live-observed fixture defect this commit fixes (.review/findings/ldt-4.md records shape-identical-but-content-different; shape drift is the adjacent case) — is discovered only after copying up to the full fixture: the launch spends a 1 GiB-plus transfer before voiding, and in the oversize case scp can push q's free space below the 33 GB MIN_FREE_BYTES floor before failing, leaving an oversized partial tree retained in the session namespace. End state is still fail-closed, so impact is wasted transfer, host disk pressure, and operator cleanup burden in already-failing scenarios.
findings.better_approach: Validate the fetched canonical manifest before copying: compute `win_shape=$(manifest_shape "$win_manifest")` immediately after the fetch and session_void unless it equals `expected_shape "$fixture"`, then run the space gate (optionally sizing it from the observed manifest byte total, which now provably equals the registered constant) before invoking scp; keep the post-copy q-versus-Windows shape and byte-for-byte cmp checks unchanged.
